017package org.apache.juneau.commons.svl;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
022
023import java.io.*;
024import java.lang.reflect.*;
025import java.util.*;
026import java.util.function.*;
027
028import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.commons.lang.*;
031
032/**
033 * A var resolver session that combines a {@link VarResolver} with one or more session objects.
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * Instances of this class are considered light-weight and fast to construct, use, and discard.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * This class contains the workhorse code for var resolution.
040 *
041 * <p>
042 * Instances of this class are created through the {@link VarResolver#createSession()} and
043 * {@link VarResolver#createSession(WritableBeanStore)} methods.
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not guaranteed to be thread safe.
047 * </ul>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
050 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SimpleVariableLanguageBasics">Simple Variable Language Basics</a>
051 * </ul>
052 */
053@SuppressWarnings({
054   "resource",   // VarResolver resources managed by calling code
055   "java:S115"   // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_contextBeanStore)
056})
057public class VarResolverSession {
058
059   // Property name constants
060   private static final String PROP_contextBeanStore = "context.beanStore";
061   private static final String PROP_sessionBeanStore = "session.beanStore";
062   private static final String PROP_var = "var";
063
064   private static boolean containsVars(Collection<?> c) {
065      var f = Flag.create();
066      c.forEach(x -> {
067         if (x instanceof CharSequence && x.toString().contains("$"))
068            f.set();
069      });
070      return f.isSet();
071   }
072
073   private static boolean containsVars(Map<?,?> m) {
074      var f = Flag.create();
075      m.forEach((k, v) -> {
076         if (v instanceof CharSequence && v.toString().contains("$"))
077            f.set();
078      });
079      return f.isSet();
080   }
081
082   private static boolean containsVars(Object array) {
083      for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(array); i++) {
084         var o = Array.get(array, i);
085         if (o instanceof CharSequence && o.toString().contains("$"))
086            return true;
087      }
088      return false;
089   }
090
091   private final VarResolver context;
092
093   private final WritableBeanStore beanStore;
094
095   /**
096    * Constructor.
097    *
098    * @param context
099    *    The {@link VarResolver} context object that contains the {@link Var Vars} and context objects associated with
100    *    that resolver.
101    * @param beanStore The bean store to use for resolving beans needed by vars.
102    *
103    */
104   public VarResolverSession(VarResolver context, WritableBeanStore beanStore) {
105      this.context = context;
106      this.beanStore = new BasicBeanStore(beanStore);
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Adds a bean to this session.
111    *
112    * @param <T> The bean type.
113    * @param c The bean type.
114    * @param value The bean.
115    * @return This object.
116    */
117   public <T> VarResolverSession bean(Class<T> c, T value) {
118      beanStore.addBean(c, value);
119      return this;
120   }
121
122   /**
123    * Returns the bean from the registered bean store.
124    *
125    * @param <T> The value type.
126    * @param c The bean type.
127    * @return
128    *    The bean.
129    *    <br>Never <jk>null</jk>.
130    */
131   public <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> c) {
132      Optional<T> t = beanStore.getBean(c);
133      if (! t.isPresent())
134         t = context.beanStore.getBean(c);
135      return t;
136   }
137
138   /**
139    * Resolve all variables in the specified string.
140    *
141    * @param s
142    *    The string to resolve variables in.
143    * @return
144    *    The new string with all variables resolved, or the same string if no variables were found.
145    *    <br>Returns <jk>null</jk> if the input was <jk>null</jk>.
146    */
147   public String resolve(String s) {
148      if (s == null)
149         return null;
150      if (s.isEmpty())
151         return s;
152      return context.compile(s).resolve(this);
153   }
154
155   /**
156    * Compiles the given template string into a reusable {@link VarTemplate}.
157    *
158    * <p>
159    * Equivalent to {@link VarResolver#compile(String) this.context.compile(input)}; provided on
160    * the session for symmetry with {@link #resolve(String)}. The returned template is bound to
161    * the underlying {@link VarResolver}, not to this session — see the {@link VarTemplate}
162    * class javadoc for the compile-binding contract.
163    *
164    * @param input The template string. May be {@code null}.
165    * @return A new {@link VarTemplate} bound to the underlying resolver.
166    */
167   public VarTemplate compile(String input) {
168      return context.compile(input);
169   }
170
171   /**
172    * Returns a session-bound {@link Supplier} that re-resolves the given template against
173    * <i>this</i> session each time {@link Supplier#get()} is called.
174    *
175    * <p>
176    * Unlike {@link VarResolver#resolveSupplier(String)} — which opens a fresh session per
177    * {@code .get()} and is threadsafe to share — this Supplier captures the existing session
178    * and reuses it. <b>It is therefore not threadsafe</b>; it inherits the standard
179    * "{@link VarResolverSession} is not guaranteed to be thread safe" contract.
180    *
181    * <p>
182    * Use this variant only when the caller owns the session lifecycle and the perf saving of
183    * skipping per-call session construction matters. For shared / cross-thread use cases,
184    * prefer {@link VarResolver#resolveSupplier(String)}.
185    *
186    * @param input The template string. May be {@code null}.
187    * @return A {@code Supplier<String>} bound to this session.
188    */
189   public Supplier<String> resolveSupplier(String input) {
190      return compile(input).asSupplier(this);
191   }
192
193   /**
194    * Resolves the specified strings in the string array.
195    *
196    * @param in The string array containing variables to resolve.
197    * @return An array with resolved strings.
198    */
199   public String[] resolve(String[] in) {
200      var out = new String[in.length];
201      for (var i = 0; i < in.length; i++)
202         out[i] = resolve(in[i]);
203      return out;
204   }
205
206   /**
207    * Convenience method for resolving variables in arbitrary objects.
208    *
209    * <p>
210    * Supports resolving variables in the following object types:
211    * <ul>
212    *    <li>{@link CharSequence}
213    *    <li>Arrays containing values of type {@link CharSequence}.
214    *    <li>Collections containing values of type {@link CharSequence}.
215    *       <br>Collection class must have a no-arg constructor.
216    *    <li>Maps containing values of type {@link CharSequence}.
217    *       <br>Map class must have a no-arg constructor.
218    * </ul>
219    *
220    * @param <T> The value type.
221    * @param o The object.
222    * @return The same object if no resolution was needed, otherwise a new object or data structure if resolution was
223    * needed.
224    */
225   @SuppressWarnings({
226      "rawtypes",   // Raw types necessary for generic resolution
227      "unchecked",  // Type erasure requires unchecked operations
228      "java:S3776"  // Cognitive complexity acceptable for recursive resolution logic
229   })
230   public <T> T resolve(T o) {
231      if (o == null)
232         return null;
233      if (o instanceof CharSequence o2)
234         return (T)resolve(o2.toString());
235      if (isArray(o)) {
236         if (! containsVars(o))
237            return o;
238         var o2 = Array.newInstance(o.getClass().getComponentType(), Array.getLength(o));
239         for (var i = 0; i < Array.getLength(o); i++)
240            Array.set(o2, i, resolve(Array.get(o, i)));
241         return (T)o2;
242      }
243      if (o instanceof Set o2) {
244         try {
245            if (! containsVars(o2))
246               return o;
247            Set o3 = info(o).getDeclaredConstructor(x -> x.isPublic() && x.getParameterCount() == 0).map(ci -> safe(() -> (Set)ci.inner().newInstance())).orElseGet(LinkedHashSet::new);
248            Set o4 = o3;
249            o2.forEach(x -> o4.add(resolve(x)));
250            return (T)o3;
251         } catch (VarResolverException e) {
252            throw e;
253         } catch (Exception e) {
254            throw new VarResolverException(e, "Problem occurred resolving set.");
255         }
256      }
257      if (o instanceof List o2) {
258         try {
259            if (! containsVars(o2))
260               return o;
261            List o3 = info(o).getDeclaredConstructor(x -> x.isPublic() && x.getParameterCount() == 0).map(ci -> safe(() -> (List)ci.inner().newInstance())).orElseGet(() -> list());
262            List o4 = o3;
263            o2.forEach(x -> o4.add(resolve(x)));
264            return (T)o3;
265         } catch (VarResolverException e) {
266            throw e;
267         } catch (Exception e) {
268            throw new VarResolverException(e, "Problem occurred resolving collection.");
269         }
270      }
271      if (o instanceof Map o2) {
272         try {
273            if (! containsVars(o2))
274               return o;
275            Map o3 = info(o).getDeclaredConstructor(x -> x.isPublic() && x.getParameterCount() == 0).map(ci -> safe(() -> (Map)ci.inner().newInstance())).orElseGet(LinkedHashMap::new);
276            Map o4 = o3;
277            o2.forEach((k, v) -> o4.put(k, resolve(v)));
278            return (T)o3;
279         } catch (VarResolverException e) {
280            throw e;
281         } catch (Exception e) {
282            throw new VarResolverException(e, "Problem occurred resolving map.");
283         }
284      }
285      return o;
286   }
287
288   /**
289    * Resolves variables in the specified string and sends the output to the specified writer.
290    *
291    * <p>
292    * More efficient than first parsing to a string and then serializing to the writer since this method doesn't need
293    * to construct a large string.
294    *
295    * @param s The string to resolve variables in.
296    * @param out The writer to write to.
297    * @return The same writer.
298    * @throws IOException Thrown by underlying stream.
299    */
300   public Writer resolveTo(String s, Writer out) throws IOException {
301      if (s == null)
302         return out;
303      if (s.isEmpty()) {
304         return out;
305      }
306      return context.compile(s).resolveTo(this, out);
307   }
308
309   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
310      // @formatter:off
311      return filteredBeanPropertyMap()
312         .a(PROP_contextBeanStore, this.context.beanStore)
313         .a(PROP_var, this.context.getVarMap().keySet())
314         .a(PROP_sessionBeanStore, beanStore);
315      // @formatter:on
316   }
317
318   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
319   public String toString() {
320      return r(properties());
321   }
322
323   /**
324    * Returns the {@link Var} with the specified name.
325    *
326    * @param name The var name (e.g. <js>"S"</js>).
327    * @return The {@link Var} instance, or <jk>null</jk> if no <c>Var</c> is associated with the specified name.
328    */
329   protected Var getVar(String name) {
330      Var v = this.context.getVarMap().get(name);
331      return nn(v) && v.canResolve(this) ? v : null;
332   }
333}
